title: Efficient Preparation of Competent Cells and Recombinant Protein Expression in E. coli BL21 (DE3) Using IPTG Induction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3iukh","text":"Reproducible method for preparing TSS-competent E. coli BL21 (DE3) cells, transforming them with plasmids, inducing protein expression using IPTG and expression analysis by SDS-PAGE. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":48,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Selection of a healthy strain of BL21 (DE3) E. coli Culture from an Agar Plate
Inoculate the isolated colony and incubate 200 rpm, 37 °C, 1 day, Overnight
Prepare 100 mL of L.B Broth 2.5 g, in 100 mL of Water
Dilute the overnight culture in a 1:100 ratio 1 mL, of overnight culture in 99 mL, Autoclaved L.B Broth
Incubate at 180 rpm, 37 °C, , Till it reaches 0.5 - 0.6 OD
After reaching 0.5 OD, cells should be arrested by incubating the culture on ice for 00:30:00, (30 Minutes)
Cells were harvested by centrifuging at 4000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes, (10 Minutes)

Prepare the TSS Solution (10 mL Total Volume) 10 % of PEG for a 10 mL solution is 1 g 5 % of DMSO for a 10 mL solution is 500 µL 50 mM MgCl2 for a 10 mL solution is 47 mg Add all the components in 8.45 g, of Autoclaved L.B Broth to make total volume of 10 mL
Discard the supernatant. Suspend the cells by adding 1 mL, of TSS Solution by tapping them to ice
After resuspension of cells, Aliquote 50 µL, in each 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes (Pre-Chilled)
Prepare 50 mL of L.B Agar and autoclaved it at 121 °C, for 15 Minutes 2 g, of L.B Agar in 50 mL Water
To check the experiment, add 50 µL, of competent cells in 25 ml Agar Plates + Kanamycin Antibiotics In this step, if we can't see any colonies, it is likely expected that competent cells are ready.
Thawed the cells by keeping the eppendorf containing competent cells at 0 °C, on ice
Add 100 ng Plasmid to the Eppendorf 100ng= 0.1 ug
Incubate the Eppendorf for 15 Minutes on Ice
Heat-shock the Eppendorf containing competent cells 42 °C for 45 Seconds
Put Eppendorf on ice for 2-4 Minutes
Add 800 µL, of L.B Broth in Eppendorf and incubate at 180 rpm, 37 °C, 1 hour This step is known as regeneration
After regeneration, harvest the cell by centrifugation 4000 rpm, 37 °C, 30 minutes
Discard the supernatant and add 200 µL, of L.B Broth , resuspend it till you see a clear solution (Don't resuspend the cells vigorously , it may damage transformant cells . )
Pour 100 µL, of Transformant cells into an agar plate (25 mL plate) and add 10 µL, Kanamycin
Make 50 mL L.B Agar and pour it into the plates (25 mL each) In the upcoming steps, this L.B Media will be used
Make 25 mL L.B Broth and store in 50 mL Falcon tube In the upcoming steps, this L.B Media will be used
Incubate it overnight at 180 rpm, 37 °C, 1 day
Colonies are likely to appear if they have an uptake of plasmid
Pick a healthy, medium-sized, isolated colony from an agar plate and inoculate it in 10 mL, of L.B Broth (Primary Culture) ; Incubate at 180 rpm, 37 °C, 1 day
Make 40 mL L.B Broth and aliquot 10 mL each in 15 mL Falcon
Take 1% 0.1 µL of the Transformant cells from an overnight culture and inoculate them into 10 mL Falcon (All 4 of them). Incubate them at 180 rpm, 37 °C, , Till it reaches 0.5 OD (Secondary culture)
Add 0.5 mM of IPTG into two Falcon tubes and 0.1 mM of IPTG into two Falcon tubes.
Incubate two Falcon tubes containing 0.1 & 0.5 mM concentration of IPTG at 25 °C 16:00:00 Incubate two Falcon tubes containing 0.1 & 0.5 mM concentration of IPTG at 37 °C 04:00:00
Harvest all 4 Falcon Tubes at 4000 rpm, 37 °C, 15 minutes (At their respective timing)
Run SDS-PAGE and check the protein expression
creator: Durvesh Burhade
